claim.summary: An RSI(2) < 10 long/flat overlay on a noise panel harvests short-horizon mean reversion net of taker cost.
claim.mechanism: A 2-period RSI below 10 marks a short-horizon oversold extreme; entering long at that close is claimed to harvest mean reversion. On this seeded synthetic noise panel the claim is FALSE BY CONSTRUCTION — this worked example exists to demonstrate the submission format, and it KILLs by design.
claim.expectedFailureMode: baselines (the mostly-flat overlay loses to buy-and-hold / equal-weight on a drifting panel) and deflated_sharpe at honest N=1 on a zero-edge series.

sources.series: 6-asset daily noise panel + RSI(2) overlay gross returns, 600 rows

notes: WORKED EXAMPLE — KILLS BY DESIGN. The panel is seeded synthetic noise (generate-stub.mjs); an oscillator overlay on noise has no edge by construction. Use this directory as the template for a real submission: replace the data with $0 public sources, keep every declaration honest.
